summary: Gruber investigates Google's Duplex AI demo by trying to identify the restaurant where Google engineers claimed to have booked a meal via the system. By posting the photo to Twitter, his followers identified the restaurant as Hong's Gourmet in Saratoga, CA within 22 minutes, using a reflected sign from the neighboring Masu Sushi visible in the photo. The exercise demonstrates both growing skepticism around Google's Duplex demo and the investigative power of a large Twitter following. Gruber notes the image was inexplicably flipped horizontally by Google, adding another odd detail to an already suspicious announcement.
key_points: Gruber crowdsourced the identification of the restaurant in Google's Duplex blog post photo to verify the demo's claims
Twitter followers identified Hong's Gourmet in Saratoga, CA in 22 minutes by matching decor and a reflected sign from the neighboring Masu Sushi
Google inexplicably flipped the photo horizontally, possibly to have the project lead's name listed first in the caption
The investigation reflects deepening skepticism about whether Google's Duplex demo was authentic or staged
Gruber reflects on the unique investigative power of having a large Twitter following for rapid crowdsourced answers
money_quotes: As suspicions around this announcement deepen, I got to wondering if we could identify this restaurant.
If we could identify the restaurant, we could ask them if they had been told in advance they would be speaking to Google Duplex, among other interesting questions.
So, I did the laziest thing I could possibly do: I asked my Twitter followers if any of them recognized it.
The fact that I had an answer to my question in just 22 minutes shows that having a large follower count on Twitter is a bit of a super power.
I honestly can't think of another way to answer this question without Google PR's help.
The threaded, public, instant nature of Twitter allowed for multiple people to contribute — we went from 'this might be the place' to 'this is definitely the place' in just a handful of minutes.
Solving this not from the decor of the restaurant but instead from the tiny reflection of the neighboring restaurant's sign brings to mind one word: 'Enhance.'
key_insight: Crowdsourced investigation via Twitter can rapidly fact-check corporate tech demos, and the details Google chose to obscure — like flipping the photo — only deepen suspicion about the authenticity of its Duplex announcement.
